title: Online Return System (ORS)
body_text:
Online Return System (ORS)
1	Introduction to ORS
1.1	Overview of ORS
The Online Return System (ORS) facilitates the return of cheques and AFT transactions. ORS also allows users to view Incoming Clearing Items (including Chargebacks), research deposit items (including deposit adjustments), reinstate cheques, and create AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Authorized users access ORS through Central 1ГўВЂВ™s Client Centre. User management for the Client Centre is managed through User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures on creating, modifying, and deleting Client Centre users.
Key Features of ORS
ORS allows authorized users to:
View Qualified Incoming Clearing Items
ГўВЂВ“ Users can search for, view, and print images of Qualified Incoming Clearing Items. Since physical items arenГўВЂВ™t available to financial institutions or their customers, use ORS to validate Qualified Incoming Clearing Items and make pay or no-pay decisions.
Note:	Unqualified Items arenГўВЂВ™t available on ORS, but copies are available for download via the File and Report Exchange. See the Incoming Clearing Items Part for more information.
View Chargebacks
ГўВЂВ“ Users can search for, view, and print images of Chargebacks.
Create Cheque Returns
ГўВЂВ“ Users can return Canadian and US dollar cheques or upload cheque return files to ORS directly. Central 1 forwards the returned cheques to the negotiating financial institutions on your financial institutionГўВЂВ™s behalf.
Create AFT Returns
ГўВЂВ“ Users can return Canadian and US dollar AFT transactions directly in ORS or upload Canadian and US dollar AFT return files to ORS.
Create AFT Notice of Change Transactions
ГўВЂВ“ Users can enter Canadian and US dollar AFT Notice of Change transactions directly in ORS or upload Canadian and US dollar AFT Notice of Change files to ORS.
View AFT Transactions
ГўВЂВ“ Users can search for, view, and print AFT transactions.
View Deposits
ГўВЂВ“ Users can search for, view, and print deposit slips and cheques within a deposit. This includes Canadian dollar Mobile Deposits, Canadian and US dollar deposits submitted via paper exchange, Branch Capture, or Corporate Capture, and foreign funds deposits sent via paper exchange to Central 1.
Note: 	Central 1 no longer accepts foreign deposits (other than USD). However, foreign deposits previously made through Central 1 are available on ORS for seven years from the date of deposit.
View and Print Deposit Adjustments
ГўВЂВ“ Users can search for, view and print deposit adjustments along with the associated deposit slip and adjusted items if applicable.
Reinstate Cheques
ГўВЂВ“ Users can update cheque data (i.e., the Transit Number, account number, or cheque number) that was incorrectly captured or that changed within your financial institutionГўВЂВ™s system (such as merged accounts). Reinstated cheques with the correct information are easier to locate on ORS (e.g., for inquiries). Reinstating cheques also improves the match rate for cheque image statements and online banking.
Search for Suspect Duplicate Items
ГўВЂВ“ If a cheque drawn on a customerГўВЂВ™s account is deposited twice within 180 days, it is listed as a suspect duplicate item. View suspected duplicate items and determine if they are duplicates. If you identify a duplicate cheque deposit, return it on ORS within 90 calendar days of the clearing date.
Identify Transactions with Colour Codes
ГўВЂВ“ ORS uses colour codes to identify cheques and AFT transactions that are eligible for return in the current processing cycle.
Use a Payments Canada-Compliant System
ГўВЂВ“ ORS prompts users to enter required fields and assists in meeting Payments Canada rules for cheque and AFT returns.
Request Reports
ГўВЂВ“ ORS provides an enhanced reporting function.
Cheque Returns
The Online Return System (ORS) Part provides procedures on how to enter cheque returns or upload cheque return files to ORS. Your financial institution is responsible for complying with all Payments Canada rules and time frames.
See the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for information on the business rules surrounding cheque returns and how to return cheques manually. Manual returns are only permitted if
the cheque is an Unqualified Item (use ORS to return Qualified Incoming Clearing Items),
the cheque cleared before images were available on ORS,
the cheque cannot be returned using ORS (such as a misdirected Chargeback), or
ORS is unavailable.
Once a dishonoured cheque is entered in ORS or a file of dishonoured cheques is uploaded to ORS, Central 1 forwards the dishonoured cheque to the negotiating financial institution using a Return Replacement Document (RRD) or an Image Printout. See Chapter 9, Returning Cheques on ORS, or Chapter 14, Uploading Files to ORS, for more information.
Changes to the
Bills of Exchange Act
and Payments Canada Rule A10 extend the same legal standing to cheque images as to physical cleared cheques.
AFT Returns
The Online Return System (ORS) Part provides procedures on how to enter AFT returns or upload files of AFT returns to ORS. additional information on returning AFT transactions in the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part. Your financial institution is responsible for complying with all Payments Canada rules and time frames.
Once a returned AFT transaction is entered on ORS or a file of returned AFT transactions is uploaded to ORS, Central 1 forwards the dishonoured AFT transaction to the originating financial institution. See Chapter 10, Returning AFT Transactions on ORS, or Chapter 14, Uploading Files to ORS, for more information.
Related Payment Solutions Manual Parts
User Management Part
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part
AFT Reports Part
Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part
1.2	ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
ORS Service Windows
ORS is available from 5:00 am to 11:00 pm PT (8:00 am to 2:00 am ET), seven days a week. However, cheques and AFT transactions can only be returned during specific service windows. ORS isnГўВЂВ™t available from 11:00 pm to 5:00 am PT (2:00 am to 8:00 am ET) for daily scheduled maintenance.
AFT returns and AFT Notice of Change transactions must be created, edited, or deleted during the following service windows:

A service window indicator is displayed on the ORS menu bar to the left of Alerts. The service window indicator only applies to cheque returns.
Regular Service (indicator is white)
Research Service (indicator is red)
Availability of Daily Cheque Images and AFT Transactions on ORS
Cheque images and AFT items are available on ORS the day following receipt at CentralВ 1.
ORS Archive of AFT Transactions and Cheque Images
AFT transactions are archived on ORS for 93 calendar days. As per Payments Canada rules, ORS doesnГўВЂВ™t permit the return of AFT transactions more than 90 calendar days after the processing date.
ORS retains a history of cheque images for seven years as follows:
BC Financial Institutions
ГўВЂВ“ The archive begins with qualified cheques cleared in January 2009 and deposits in January 2008.
Ontario Financial Institutions ГўВЂВ“
The archive begins with deposits on March 31, 2012, qualified Canadian dollar cheques cleared on April 2, 2012, and qualified US dollar cheques cleared on April 5, 2011.
Note:	To return a cheque that cleared before Incoming Clearing Items were added to the ORS archive, follow the manual cheque returns procedures in the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part.
